# @sunacchi/pila

> **Pieces of Information, Linked and Arranged** — a framework-agnostic, Notion-style block editor in TypeScript.
> Pure Web Components. No React / Vue / Svelte dependency. Full TypeScript types included.
> npm: `@sunacchi/pila` | Repo: https://github.com/juanchinovas/pila

## Install

```bash
npm install @sunacchi/pila
```

```ts
import { PilaEditor } from '@sunacchi/pila'
import '@sunacchi/pila/styles'          // required — import once in your entry point
```

## Core usage

```ts
const editor = new PilaEditor(document.getElementById('editor'), {
  placeholder: 'Type / to add a block…',  // shown in empty first block
  initialContent: [],                      // Block[] — pre-populate content
  onChange(blocks) {},                     // called on every content change
  plugins: [],                             // PilaPlugin[] — optional plugins
})

editor.mount()    // renders editor into the container
editor.destroy()  // unmounts and cleans up all resources
```

## Export content

```ts
editor.getContent('json')      // → string  canonical Block[] JSON (round-trips back into editor)
editor.getContent('html')      // → string  semantic HTML (display only, cannot feed back)
editor.getContent('markdown')  // → string  GFM Markdown
editor.getContent('email')     // → string  standalone <!DOCTYPE html> email (inline styles, Outlook-safe)
```

Restore from JSON:

```ts
new PilaEditor(el, { initialContent: JSON.parse(editor.getContent('json')) })
```

## Content model

```ts
interface Block {
  id: string             // unique string id
  type: BlockType        // see block types below
  content?: InlineNode[] // rich-text content
  attrs?: BlockAttrs     // block-specific attributes
}

interface InlineNode {
  text: string
  bold?: boolean
  italic?: boolean
  underline?: boolean
  code?: boolean
  link?: string          // http / https / mailto only
}
```

## Block types

All 14 built-in `type` values:

| type | Description | Key attrs |
|---|---|---|
| `paragraph` | Plain text | — |
| `heading1` `heading2` `heading3` | `<h1>`–`<h3>` | — |
| `bulletList` | Unordered list item | — |
| `numberedList` | Ordered list item | — |
| `todo` | Checkbox item | `checked: boolean` |
| `code` | Fenced code block (Prism.js, 16 languages) | `language: string` |
| `quote` | Blockquote | — |
| `callout` | Highlighted callout box | `icon: string`, `flavor: 'info'\|'warning'\|'error'\|'success'\|'tip'` |
| `divider` | Horizontal rule (`<hr>`) | — |
| `image` | Image with caption and props modal | `src`, `alt`, `width`, `height`, `alignment`, `tailwindClasses` |
| `table` | Data table | `rows: TableRow[]`, `headerRows: number[]`, `headerCols: number[]` |
| `columns` | Multi-column layout with nested blocks | `columnDefs: ColumnDef[]` |
| `button` | Clickable button | `href: string`, `buttonStyle: 'primary'\|'secondary'\|'outline'` |

### `code` language values

`plaintext` `bash` `css` `csharp` `java` `javascript` `json` `jsx` `markdown` `markup` `python` `sql` `tsx` `typescript` `yaml`

Aliases resolved automatically: `js`→`javascript`, `ts`→`typescript`, `py`→`python`, `sh`/`shell`/`zsh`→`bash`, `html`/`xml`/`svg`→`markup`, `yml`→`yaml`.

### Table structure

```ts
interface TableRow  { cells: TableCell[] }
interface TableCell { content: InlineNode[]; align?: 'left' | 'center' | 'right' }
```

### Columns structure

```ts
interface ColumnDef { width?: number; blocks: Block[] }  // width = flex-grow, default 1
```

## Plugin API

```ts
import type { PilaPlugin } from '@sunacchi/pila'

const plugin: PilaPlugin = {
  name: 'my-plugin',
  install(api) {
    // Register a custom block type
    api.registerBlockType({
      type: 'my-card',
      factory(block) {
        const el = document.createElement('div')
        el.textContent = block.attrs?.title ?? ''
        return el
      },
      slashItem: { name: 'Card', description: 'Insert a card', icon: '🃏' },
    })

    // Add a floating toolbar button
    api.addToolbarButton({
      label: 'Hi',
      title: 'Say hi',
      command() { alert('hello') },
    })

    // Subscribe to editor events
    api.on('blocks:change', ({ blocks }) => console.log(blocks))
  },
}

new PilaEditor(el, { plugins: [plugin] }).mount()
```

### PilaPluginAPI members

| Member | Description |
|---|---|
| `editorEl` | Root editor DOM element |
| `manager` | `BlockManager` — read/write access to block state |
| `registerBlockType(descriptor)` | Register a custom block type with optional slash item |
| `addToolbarButton(descriptor)` | Add a button to the floating formatting toolbar |
| `on(event, handler)` | Subscribe to editor events; returns an unsubscribe fn |

### Editor events

| Event | Payload |
|---|---|
| `block:add` | `{ block: Block; index: number }` |
| `block:update` | `{ id: string; block: Block }` |
| `block:delete` | `{ id: string }` |
| `block:move` | `{ id: string; toIndex: number }` |
| `blocks:change` | `{ blocks: Block[] }` |

## Theming — CSS custom properties

Override on `:root` (global) or any ancestor selector (scoped). Import source variables with:

```ts
import '@sunacchi/pila/styles/source'
```

Full variable list (from `src/styles/variables.css`):

```css
:root {
  --pila-font: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', 'Roboto', sans-serif;
  --pila-mono: 'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', 'Consolas', 'Monaco', monospace;

  --pila-bg: #ffffff;
  --pila-text: #1a1a1a;
  --pila-muted: #9b9b9b;
  --pila-placeholder: #c4c4c4;
  --pila-accent: #2563eb;
  --pila-accent-hover: #1d4ed8;
  --pila-border: #e2e8f0;
  --pila-radius: 6px;
  --pila-shadow: 0 4px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.10);

  --pila-code-bg: #f1f5f9;
  --pila-code-text: #0f172a;
  --pila-inline-code-bg: #f1f5f9;
  --pila-inline-code-text: #0f172a;

  /* callout flavors: info / warning / error / success / tip */
  --pila-callout-info-bg:       #eff6ff;
  --pila-callout-info-border:   #2563eb;
  --pila-callout-info-text:     #1e3a8a;
  --pila-callout-warning-bg:    #fffbeb;
  --pila-callout-warning-border: #d97706;
  --pila-callout-warning-text:  #78350f;
  --pila-callout-error-bg:      #fef2f2;
  --pila-callout-error-border:  #dc2626;
  --pila-callout-error-text:    #7f1d1d;
  --pila-callout-success-bg:    #f0fdf4;
  --pila-callout-success-border: #16a34a;
  --pila-callout-success-text:  #14532d;
  --pila-callout-tip-bg:        #faf5ff;
  --pila-callout-tip-border:    #9333ea;
  --pila-callout-tip-text:      #3b0764;

  --pila-quote-border: #94a3b8;
  --pila-quote-text:   #4b5563;

  --pila-toolbar-bg:   #1e1e1e;
  --pila-toolbar-text: #f8f8f8;

  --pila-slash-bg:       #ffffff;
  --pila-slash-border:   #e2e8f0;
  --pila-slash-hover:    #f1f5f9;
  --pila-slash-selected: #eff6ff;
}
```

## Serializers (standalone use)

```ts
import { JsonSerializer, HtmlSerializer, MarkdownSerializer, EmailSerializer } from '@sunacchi/pila'

JsonSerializer.serialize(blocks)    // Block[] → JSON string
HtmlSerializer.serialize(blocks)    // Block[] → semantic HTML string
MarkdownSerializer.serialize(blocks) // Block[] → GFM Markdown string
EmailSerializer.serialize(blocks)   // Block[] → standalone <!DOCTYPE html> email string
```

## Framework integration

Mount after the element is in the DOM; destroy before it is removed.

**React**

```tsx
useEffect(() => {
  const editor = new PilaEditor(ref.current!, options)
  editor.mount()
  return () => editor.destroy()
}, [])
```

**Vue**

```ts
onMounted(() => { editor = new PilaEditor(el.value!, options); editor.mount() })
onBeforeUnmount(() => editor?.destroy())
```

**Svelte**

```ts
onMount(() => { editor = new PilaEditor(el, options); editor.mount() })
onDestroy(() => editor?.destroy())
```

## Source file map

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `src/types.ts` | All TypeScript interfaces and types |
| `src/index.ts` | Public exports |
| `src/core/Editor.ts` | `PilaEditor` class |
| `src/core/BlockManager.ts` | Block state management |
| `src/styles/variables.css` | All CSS custom properties |
| `src/blocks/README.md` | Per-block-type schema reference |
| `src/serializers/` | JSON / HTML / Markdown / Email serializers |
